Revision 27-02-2026:
- Updated domain definitions of properties with era:rinfIndex in the range 1.1.1.3.2.1–1.1.1.3.2.15 and 1.1.1.3.2.16.1–1.1.1.3.2.16.13 (excluding 1.1.1.3.2.16).
- Preserved existing owl:unionOf domain expressions and replaced occurrences of era:Track or era:RunningTrack within those unions with era:ETCS.
- Change rdfs:range of era:canonicalURI to rdfs:Resource

Revision 26-02-2026:
- Moved rdfs:label SHACL constraint to era:InfrastructureElement level, removing redundant per-subclass bindings (Bridge, LevelCrossing, SpecialArea, SpecialTunnelArea, Switch) and replacing the dedicated LinearPositioningSystem Label shape with the generic RdfsLabel shape.
- Added RINF concept era-lrs:100 \"None\" and renamed era-lrs-eratv:None prefLabel to \"None - eratv\" to disambiguate RINF and ERATV None concepts in LegacyRadioSystems.

Revision 25-02-2026:
- Restricted MaxPermittedTrainLengthApplicability SPARQL constraint to only check tracks that are part of a era:SectionOfLine (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/285).
- Corrected URIs of referenced concept schemes from the ERA Ontology.
- Corrected URIs of referenced shapes from the ERA Ontology.
- Added missing backward relationships to ERA Ontology from shapes.

Revision 24-02-2026:
- Removed dcterms:relation to non existing era:bridgeWindRestriction.
- Fixed REGEX type error in SKOS concept scheme SPARQL constraints: wrapped IRI-typed ?concept in STR() so REGEX receives an xsd:string as required by SPARQL 1.1 (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/276).
- Fixed SolNatureApplicability SPARQL constraint to also match tracks linked via era:hasPart (not only deprecated era:track) (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/275).
- Fixed EddyCurrentBrakingConditionsDocumentApplicability SPARQL constraint to use concept URIs from the eddy-current-braking scheme instead of magnetic-braking (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/273).
- Fixed NoRepeatedTrackIdsSoL SPARQL constraint: removed wrongful a era:OperationalPoint type checks, as the constraint targets era:SectionOfLine subjects (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/270).
- Added \"passenger stop\" (era-op-types:70) to the exclusion list in HasPartTrackOP SPARQL constraint (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/269).
- Removed owl:FunctionalProperty from era:lineReferenceTunnelStart and era:lineReferenceTunnelEnd, and removed the corresponding sh:maxCount 1 SHACL constraints, as tunnels may span multiple tracks (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/263).

Revision 23-02-2026:
- Fixed SPARQL constraint messages: ensure ?label-like variables are always bound using COALESCE, preventing message construction failures when nolabel exists (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/264).
- Added ?path binding to all SPARQL constraint SELECT clauses, enabling SHACL result paths to be reported in validation results (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/265).
- Moved sh:severity from sh:SPARQLConstraint instances to sh:NodeShape definitions to comply with the SHACL specification (domain of sh:severity is sh:Shape). Created dedicated *SPARQLWarning NodeShapes for constraints with sh:Warning severity (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/280).
- Fixed conditionsAppliedRegenerativeBraking applicability constraint to also check the conditionalRegenerativeBrake value, excluding cases where regenerative braking is not applicable or not present (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/268).
- Fixed NoRepeatedPlatformIdsOP SPARQL constraint to correctly detect duplicate platformId values (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/271).
- Fixed NoRepeatedSidingIds SPARQL constraint to correctly detect duplicate sidingId values (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/272).
- Fixed HABD SPARQL constraints: added missing $this in SELECT clauses (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/274).
- Fixed SoLTrackDirectionMaxCount SPARQL constraint to use STR() for IRI comparison in duplicate track direction filter (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/277).
- Fixed unbound variables in SPARQL constraint SELECT clauses: bound ?uopid in ReferenceBorderPointApplicability, removed unbound ?value from NoRepeatedTrackIdsSoL, and fixed ?opLabel to ?slLabel with COALESCE in SubsidiaryLocationTypeSKOS (see https://gitlab.com/era-europa-eu/public/interoperable-data-programme/era-ontology/era-ontology/-/issues/278).

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/CABRestriction
http://data.europa.eu/949/NetRelation
Value:
Connectivity and the navigability between consecutive linear elements are resolved by using net relations. It models how different elements are related to each other, representing a transition from one element to another.
Net relations specify where two linear elements connect. This could be at the origin of one element, the end of another, or even at specific offsets along their lengths. For example, two tracks might connect at a junction, with one ending at offset 1500 and the other beginning at offset 0.
NetRelation explicitly models these connections by linking two linear elements (elementA and elementB) and defining their physical or logical relationship.
Net relations also specify the permitted directions of travel:
•	AB: Trains can move from element A to element B.
•	BA: Trains can move from element B to element A.
•	Both: Movement is bidirectional.
•	None: No movement is allowed between the two elements.
To make this even more precise, net relations include information about whether the connection is at the origin of each element. For example, a relation might indicate that Track A ends at its origin (offset 0) and connects to Track B at its start (isOnOriginOfElementA and isOnOriginOfElementB).
@en

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/VehicleType
Value:
A vehicle type that has been authorized to operate on the EU railway infrastructure. Type means a vehicle type defining the basic design characteristics of the vehicle as covered by a type or design examination certificate described in the relevant verification module.

Basic design characteristics means the parameters that are used to identify the vehicle type as specified in the issued vehicle type authorisation and recorded in the European Register of Authorised Vehicle Types ('ERATV').

The above mentioned certificate should be documented using era:certificate.
@en

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/demonstrationINF
Value:
EI declaration of demonstration (as defined Commission 2014/881/EU) for track relating to compliance with the requirements from TSIs applicable to infrastructure subsystem.

It may happen that several EI declarations were issued - then parameter has to be repeated as many times as many declarations were issued.

The procedure for demonstration that existing network fits to requirements of the TSIs is executed on voluntary basis, so when EI declaration do not exist then the parameter is optional. If EI declaration was not issued, then field shall be left empty. @en

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/gsmROptionalFunctions
Value:
(*1) These inputs refer to the expected behaviour by the network, i.e. if there is any area or point where an automatic selection of network should be done or if there is any location where balises to instruct a change of radio network have been installed.
In order to be able to attend to these indications (automatic network, network change by balise) some configuration is needed in the mobile.
In case there is a balise used to announce the change of the network, or if there are locations where the network selection is planned by the IM to be done automatically (and not manually, as stated in the requirements). It should be considered as an item that is related to the design of the infrastructure.

(*2) the possibility to dial 112 is something specific to the network that should be communicated to the vehicles accessing it.

(*3) the use of broadcast calls is something specific to the network that has to be configured in it.

(*4) it is something specific to the network that has to be configured in it if the service is provided.

(*5) it is something specific to the network that has to be configured in it if the service is provided; something may need to be configured on the network but also in the mobile subscriber data if it wants to use the service. What is requested here is the information of the network capability.

(*6) if they are configured on the network. Please indicate which in the \"Other information\" box.

(*7) To be selected if other data applications, different from ETCS L2, can be used within the network -

(*8) if it is configured on the network.
 \"GSM-R Shunting used\" in order to make public if the GSM-R is used in the network for shunting activities.

(*9) Please specify in the \"Other information\" box for which services /applications are they planned and which are the frequencies in use.

(*11) Please use this field to indicate any additional information on network characteristics, e.g.; interference level, leading to the need of additional on-board protection;
@en

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/opEnd
Value:
Each SoL may have only one end OP, and each OP has unique OP ID within the MS. The “uniqueOPID” is defined in parameter 1.2.0.0.0.2.
Each SoL has the principal direction of the traffic defined by increasing kilometres running from the start OP to the end OP.
That is why the end OP is always located at highest kilometre of the line within the SoL.
No validation will be performed by RINF application regarding which is the start and which the end OP. This requires IM's verification. @en

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/opName
Value: Name of OP may not always exist in common use. In such case IM should propose a name for OP. @en

Subject: http://data.europa.eu/949/opStart
Value:
Each SoL may have only one start OP, and each OP has unique OP ID within the MS. The “uniqueOPID” is defined in parameter 1.2.0.0.0.2.
Each SoL has the principal direction of the traffic defined by increasing kilometres running from the start OP to the end OP.
That is why the start OP is always located at lowest kilometre of the line within the SoL.
No validation will be performed by RINF application regarding which is the start and which the end OP. This requires IM's verification. @en
